originalAlarm.description: Memory dynamically allocated by `malloc`, indirectly via call to `prepend_new_eth_table()` on line 40 is not freed after the last access at line 40, column 3.

reasoning: Known bug for bc0ffc0e971.c is use-after-free in cleanup loop (PRODUCT_40: TRUE, unix.Malloc at L43, CLEAN_UP=T). This alarm detects a memory leak when CLEAN_UP=F: ch = malloc() at L29 is allocated but the cleanup block (L43-47) is not compiled when CLEAN_UP=F, so ch is never freed. New bug. PC (CLEAN_UP=F) exactly captures when the leak occurs. Correctly constrained.
